core_modules.name: cost_optimization/__init__.py
core_modules.responsibility: Module initialization and exports for all optimization tiers

core_modules.name: cost_optimization/prompt_cache.py
core_modules.responsibility: Prompt caching for 80-90% input cost reduction

core_modules.name: cost_optimization/docker_sandbox.py
core_modules.responsibility: Secure code execution in Docker containers

critical_paths.description: Cached API calls with cost tracking

failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Docker not available, fallback to subprocess with untrusted code
failure_scenarios.evidence: Lines 175-200: When Docker unavailable, falls back to subprocess.run() with NO security isolation. Code executes directly on host with full permissions. Line 188: subprocess created without security limits (no sandboxing, no resource limits, no network isolation).
failure_scenarios.description: SECURITY VULNERABILITY: When Docker is unavailable, DockerSandbox falls back to subprocess execution with ZERO security isolation. Malicious generated code (rm -rf /, network exfiltration, etc.) executes directly on host. This defeats the entire purpose of the sandbox.

failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: GitHub token logged or exposed in error messages
failure_scenarios.evidence: Line 365: remote_url = f'https://{self.token}@{self.base_url}...' - Token embedded in URL. Line 372: subprocess.run with capture_output=True may capture token in stderr. Line 157: Commit metadata may include sensitive information. No token redaction in error handling.
failure_scenarios.description: SECURITY RISK: GitHub token embedded in remote URL may be exposed in logs, error messages, or process listings. Token should be passed via environment variable or credential helper, not embedded in URL.

failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Workspace cleanup fails, temporary files accumulate
failure_scenarios.evidence: Line 145: workspace = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp()). Line 168-172: Cleanup in finally block but shutil.rmtree may fail silently. Line 208: _workspaces dict tracks workspaces but no periodic cleanup. No maximum workspace limit.
failure_scenarios.description: RESOURCE LEAK: Temporary workspaces created for each execution may not be cleaned up if shutil.rmtree fails. Over time, this accumulates disk usage. No maximum limit on workspace count or size.

failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Pydantic not installed, structured output fails silently
failure_scenarios.evidence: Lines 25-30: HAS_PYDANTIC check with ImportError. Line 32: logger.warning but no exception raised. Lines 100-105: __init__ checks HAS_PYDANTIC but raises ImportError. Inconsistent error handling - sometimes warns, sometimes raises.
failure_scenarios.description: INCONSISTENT ERROR HANDLING: Pydantic import failure handled inconsistently. Module-level check logs warning but doesn't prevent import. Class __init__ raises ImportError. Users may get confusing errors depending on when they instantiate.

failure_scenarios.trigger_condition: Concurrent writes to eval dataset JSONL file
failure_scenarios.evidence: Line 230: Opens file with 'a' mode and writes JSON. No file locking. Line 215: _cases list modified without lock. Multiple concurrent failures could corrupt JSONL file with interleaved writes.
failure_scenarios.description: RACE CONDITION: EvalDatasetBuilder._persist_case() appends to JSONL file without file locking. Concurrent test failures (parallel task execution) could cause interleaved writes, corrupting the dataset file.
